verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (BiP/GRP78) is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone with no membrane-anchoring domain of its own. However, it has a well-documented surface pool on tumor cells and under ER stress. Cancer cells display HSPA5 on the outer leaflet via ER stress-induced externalization and autophagy/lysosomal exocytosis, where it acts as an immunogenic antigen and damage-associated molecular pattern. This cell-state-induced surface pool is distinct from its dominant ER-luminal home and qualifies as contextual surface accessibility via cancer-cell ecto-presentation and stress-state translocation.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Uncertainty whether the documented tumor-cell and ER-stress ecto-pool of HSPA5 is stable/wash-resistant enough to count as true surface accessibility vs. transient exposure during cell death or surface sampling. Clinical CAR-T programs targeting HSPA5 suggest functional surface display, but the baseline compartment is strictly ER-luminal, making this a clear contextual call rather than yes.
